qa_pairs.question: How should a user properly terminate an RStudio session within JupyterLab to ensure that computational resources are released?
qa_pairs.answer: To properly terminate an RStudio session and release associated resources such as CPU, memory, or GPU, as well as ending the underlying Slurm job, users must go to the JupyterLab browser tab and use the menu item `File > Log Out`. Simply quitting RStudio or closing the RStudio or JupyterHub browser tabs will not achieve this.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary function of OpenRefine when launched from JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenRefine, accessible through JupyterLab, is a powerful, free, and open-source tool designed to help users clean, transform, and enhance messy data. It is frequently employed for tasks like correcting typos in manually collected survey data.

qa_pairs.question: When launching applications like LibreQDA, OpenRefine, or desktop environments from JupyterLab, what is the typical startup time and behaviour regarding session continuity?
qa_pairs.answer: For a new session of applications such as LibreQDA, OpenRefine, or the Mate and XFCE Desktop environments, startup can take up to 3 minutes. Users can typically reopen an active session for these applications even after the web browser tab has been closed, and the application session will conclude when the overall JupyterLab session ends.
